text.title: Audience Driven Pivot Decision Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Audience Driven Pivot Decision application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Operator MUST own a verifiable audience asset (list / community / customer base) ≥500. Pivot without an audience is repivot; route to that methodology.
rule.rationale: Audience-as-moat research — anchors Audience Driven Pivot Decision rule `audience-asset-required`.

rule.statement: Pivot MUST cite ≥3 distinct unsolved jobs surfaced in customer interviews within the last 90 days. No interviews = no pivot.
rule.rationale: Continuous discovery — anchors Audience Driven Pivot Decision rule `evidence-of-unsolved-jobs`.

rule.statement: The old product's kill criterion MUST be written + dated. The pivot does not start until the old product hits the kill condition or the operator commits to dual-running with a sunset date.
rule.rationale: Decision-record discipline — anchors Audience Driven Pivot Decision rule `explicit-kill-criterion-old-product`.

rule.statement: ≥4 weeks of validation (interviews + smoke pages + waitlist conversion) MUST complete before any new product code is written. Build-first pivots fail.
rule.rationale: MVP validation research — anchors Audience Driven Pivot Decision rule `validation-before-build`.

rule.statement: The decision-record MUST name the audience cohort that will follow into the new product (size, segment, source). 'Existing audience' is too vague.
rule.rationale: Cohort-segmentation discipline — anchors Audience Driven Pivot Decision rule `named-surviving-cohort`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
